<a name="Clamp"></a>
## func Clamp

```go
func Clamp[T cmp.Ordered](value, minVal, maxVal T) T
```

Clamp constrains a value to be within the range \[minVal, maxVal\].

<a name="EscapeShellArg"></a>
## func EscapeShellArg

```go
func EscapeShellArg(s string) string
```

EscapeShellArg escapes a string for safe display in shell\-like log output.

<a name="EscapeShellArg2"></a>
## func EscapeShellArg2

```go
func EscapeShellArg2(s string, _ int) string
```

EscapeShellArg2 escapes a string for safe display in shell\-like log output. The second parameter is ignored \(present for lo.Map compatibility\).

<a name="IfElse"></a>
## func IfElse

```go
func IfElse[T any](condition bool, ifTrue T, ifFalse T) T
```

IfElse returns ifTrue if condition is true, otherwise returns ifFalse.

<a name="Iff"></a>
## func Iff

```go
func Iff[T any](condition bool, ifTrue T) T
```

Iff returns ifTrue if condition is true, or T's null value

<a name="KillListeners"></a>
## func KillListeners

```go
func KillListeners(addr string)
```

KillListeners sends SIGTERM to all processes listening on the given address, waits up to 3 seconds, then sends SIGKILL to any that remain.

<a name="Null"></a>
## func Null

```go
func Null[T any]() T
```



<a name="Partition"></a>
## func Partition

```go
func Partition[T any](collection []T, predicate func(T) bool) ([]T, []T)
```

Partition splits a slice into two slices based on a predicate. The first slice contains elements for which the predicate returns true, the second contains elements for which it returns false.

<a name="ReplacePlaceholders"></a>
## func ReplacePlaceholders

```go
func ReplacePlaceholders(s string, args map[string]string, placeholderFormat string) (string, error)
```

ReplacePlaceholders replaces all placeholders in s with their values from args. placeholderFormat is a regexp with exactly one capture group identifying the key, e.g. \`\{\{\(\[^\{\]\+\)\}\}\` or \`\\$\\\{\(\[^\}\]\+\)\\\}\`. Returns an error if s contains a placeholder whose key is not in args.

<a name="Reverse"></a>
## func Reverse

```go
func Reverse[S ~[]E, E any](s S)
```

Reverse reverses a slice in place

<a name="SortBy"></a>
## func SortBy

```go
func SortBy[T any, K cmp.Ordered](collection []T, iteratee func(T) K) []T
```

SortBy returns a sorted copy of the slice, sorted by the key returned by the iteratee.

<a name="FileWatcher"></a>
## type FileWatcher

FileWatcher watches a single file for changes.

```go
type FileWatcher struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewFileWatcher"></a>
### func NewFileWatcher

```go
func NewFileWatcher(filePath string, debounceMs int) (*FileWatcher, error)
```

NewFileWatcher creates a watcher that monitors a single file for changes.

<a name="FileWatcher.Changes"></a>
### func \(\*FileWatcher\) Changes

```go
func (fw *FileWatcher) Changes() <-chan struct{}
```

Changes returns a channel that receives notifications when the file changes.

<a name="FileWatcher.Close"></a>
### func \(\*FileWatcher\) Close

```go
func (fw *FileWatcher) Close() error
```

Close stops the watcher and releases resources.

<a name="FileWatcher.Path"></a>
### func \(\*FileWatcher\) Path

```go
func (fw *FileWatcher) Path() string
```

Path returns the path of the file being watched.

<a name="LRUCache"></a>
## type LRUCache

LRUCache is a simple LRU cache using a map and slice. It includes a creator function that is called when a key is not found.

```go
type LRUCache[K comparable, V any] struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewLRUCache"></a>
### func NewLRUCache

```go
func NewLRUCache[K comparable, V any](limit int, creator func(K) (V, error)) *LRUCache[K, V]
```

NewLRUCache creates a new LRU cache with the specified limit and creator function. The creator function is called when Get is called with a key that doesn't exist. Panics if limit \< 1.

<a name="LRUCache[K, V].Get"></a>
### func \(\*LRUCache\[K, V\]\) Get

```go
func (c *LRUCache[K, V]) Get(key K) (V, error)
```

Get retrieves a value from the cache, creating it if it doesn't exist. If the key exists, it is moved to most recently used position. If the key doesn't exist, the creator function is called and the result is cached. If the creator returns an error, the result is not cached and the error is returned.
